---
title: "Research"
---

## Working Papers

*Coming soon.*

---

## Publications

**Revisiting the Environmental Kuznets Curve: Evidence from West Africa**
<br>with Yao Silvère Konan
<br>*Environmental Economics*, Vol. 12(1), 2021, pp. 64–75
<br>DOI: [10.21511/ee.12(1).2021.06](http://dx.doi.org/10.21511/ee.12(1).2021.06)

<details>
<summary>Abstract</summary>
This paper examines the relationship between income and pollution levels by testing the Environmental Kuznets Curve hypothesis in West African nations over 1980–2014. Using GDP per capita as the income measure and analyzing three pollutants (CO₂, nitrogen oxide, and methane emissions), and employing both parametric and non-parametric methods, we find an inverted-U relationship between income and methane as well as nitrogen dioxide emissions. Results for CO₂ are mixed depending on methodology. Overall, the EKC hypothesis is validated for West African countries.
</details>
